pois.name: Haines Cruise Ship Dock
pois.notes: Small dock at the edge of downtown. Walking distance to all town attractions.

pois.name: Fort William H. Seward
pois.notes: National Historic Landmark. Alaska's first permanent military post (1904). Now galleries, brewery, totem carving.

pois.name: Downtown Haines
pois.notes: Authentic small-town Alaska. Bamboo Room restaurant, local shops, and friendly residents.

pois.name: Chilkat Bald Eagle Preserve
pois.notes: 48,000-acre preserve, world's largest bald eagle gathering site. Peak Oct-Feb; good viewing year-round.

pois.name: Rainbow Glacier
pois.notes: Visible glacier in the Chilkat Range. Accessible by flightseeing tour.

pois.name: Sheldon Museum & Cultural Center
pois.notes: Local history museum covering Tlingit culture, Gold Rush era, and Chilkat blanket weaving.

pois.name: Haines Brewing Company
pois.notes: Small craft brewery with local ales. Friendly small-town atmosphere.

pois.name: Battery Point Trail
pois.notes: Easy coastal hike with beach access and Lynn Canal views. About 2 miles round trip.

pois.name: Fort Seward Parade Ground
pois.notes: Central green of the historic military post. Galleries, brewery, Alaska Indian Arts workshop.

pois.name: Chilkat Inlet
pois.notes: Protected inlet with mountain panoramas and seasonal whale sightings.
